skill.name: aws-compliance-checker
skill.description: Automated compliance checking against CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2 benchmarks
skill.summary: Automated compliance checking against CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2 benchmarks
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 83 potential issues but manual review confirms all are false positives. External commands are legitimate AWS CLI invocations for compliance checks. Network patterns are standard CIDR notation (0.0.0.0/0) for security group auditing and legitimate documentation URLs. C2 keywords and weak crypto flags are triggered by normal security compliance terminology. This is a defensive security tool for AWS compliance auditing.

high_findings.title: External Command Execution (False Positive)
high_findings.description: 42 instances of shell command execution flagged by static analyzer. All are legitimate AWS CLI commands used for compliance auditing. The skill provides example bash scripts that use 'aws' CLI to check IAM, CloudTrail, Security Groups, etc. This is expected functionality for a compliance checking tool.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations contain aws CLI commands in legitimate compliance checking scripts. Commands like 'aws iam get-credential-report', 'aws cloudtrail describe-trails', 'aws ec2 describe-security-groups' are standard AWS API calls for auditing.

high_findings.title: C2 Keywords Flag (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'C2 keywords' at multiple lines. Manual review shows these are normal security compliance terms like 'check', 'command', 'control', 'console' used in legitimate compliance checking context.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines contain standard security terms like 'check', 'console', 'command' in legitimate compliance script context. No actual C2 malware indicators present.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple lines. Manual review shows these are recommendations for strong encryption (TLS 1.2+, proper encryption) in compliance checks.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines contain compliance recommendations for strong encryption (TLS 1.2+, encrypted volumes, KMS keys). The analyzer incorrectly flags encryption-related terms in security compliance context.

medium_findings.title: Network URL References
medium_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs at lines 514-516 point to CIS, AWS Security Hub, and AWS Compliance documentation. These are legitimate reference links, not malicious network activity.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are official AWS and CIS documentation links - legitimate reference materials for compliance frameworks.

medium_findings.title: CIDR Notation in Security Group Checks
medium_findings.description: Lines 277-293 contain 0.0.0.0/0 CIDR notation used to detect overly permissive security groups. This is standard compliance checking practice, not actual hardcoded IPs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: 0.0.0.0/0 is standard CIDR notation meaning 'any IP address' used in security group compliance checks to identify rules allowing public access.

content.user_title: Check AWS Compliance Against Industry Standards
content.value_statement: Manually auditing AWS environments against CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2 benchmarks is time-consuming and error-prone. The AWS Compliance Checker skill automates compliance validation by running AWS API checks and generating detailed compliance reports.
actual_capabilities: Run CIS AWS Foundations compliance checks across IAM, logging, monitoring, and networking
Validate PCI-DSS requirements including network security, encryption, and access controls
Check HIPAA compliance for healthcare workloads covering access controls and encryption
Generate automated compliance reports with pass/fail status and scores
Audit security groups for overly permissive rules (0.0.0.0/0 access)
Verify CloudTrail, AWS Config, and VPC Flow Logs are properly configured
limitations: Requires valid AWS credentials with read-only access to IAM, EC2, CloudTrail, and other services
Does not automatically remediate findings - only detects and reports
Does not cover all controls in each framework - provides representative checks
Results depend on AWS API permissions and may miss resources in unsupported regions

use_cases.title: Pre-Audit Compliance Validation
use_cases.description: Run full compliance checks before external audits to identify and fix issues proactively.
use_cases.target_user: Security engineers and compliance officers preparing for audits

use_cases.title: Continuous Compliance Monitoring
use_cases.description: Integrate into CI/CD pipelines or scheduled jobs to maintain ongoing compliance posture.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps and platform teams managing AWS infrastructure

use_cases.title: Multi-Framework Compliance Reporting
use_cases.description: Generate unified reports covering CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2 in a single run.
use_cases.target_user: Cloud architects and security leaders needing comprehensive compliance views

prompt_templates.title: Run CIS Benchmark Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run CIS AWS Foundations compliance check on my AWS account
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick compliance validation against CIS benchmark

prompt_templates.title: Generate PCI-DSS Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a PCI-DSS compliance report for my AWS environment

prompt_templates.title: Check HIPAA Compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: Check HIPAA compliance for my AWS account, focusing on encryption and access controls

prompt_templates.title: Audit Security Groups
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit all security groups in my AWS account for overly permissive rules and generate a report

best_practices: Run compliance checks regularly (weekly or monthly) to catch configuration drift early
Use AWS Organizations to run checks across all accounts in your organization
Document exceptions and remediation plans for any failed checks
Integrate with AWS Security Hub for centralized compliance dashboards
anti_patterns: Running checks only once before audits instead of continuously monitoring
Ignoring warnings about unused credentials or overly permissive access
Relying solely on automated checks without manual security reviews
Not maintaining evidence documentation for audit trails

faq.question: What AWS permissions are needed to run these checks?
faq.answer: The skill requires read-only access to IAM, EC2, CloudTrail, CloudWatch, S3, and AWS Config. Use IAM read-only policies or the SecurityAudit AWS managed policy.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically fix compliance issues?
faq.answer: No, this skill only detects and reports compliance issues. Remediation requires manual action or separate automation scripts.

faq.question: Which compliance frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Currently supports CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2. Each framework has representative checks covering key requirements.

faq.question: Can I run checks across multiple AWS accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use AWS Organizations with cross-account roles or run the checks separately in each account and aggregate results.

faq.question: How often should compliance checks be run?
faq.answer: Best practice is to run checks at least weekly, with continuous monitoring via AWS Config Rules for production environments.

faq.question: What happens if AWS API calls fail during a check?
faq.answer: The check will report partial results with error messages indicating which checks could not complete due to permission issues or service unavailability.
